title: Call for caution on the roads this Heritage Day
description: NATIONAL NEWS - As South Africans across the...
bodyText:
NATIONAL NEWS - As South Africans across the country come together to celebrate Heritage Day, traffic authorities and law enforcement agencies are stepping up efforts to ensure road safety and public order during the long weekend.


> In a statement released today, national and provincial traffic officials emphasized their continued commitment to maintaining a visible presence on the roads to promote compliance with traffic regulations and to act against lawbreakers.


"Law enforcement agencies across the country will be out in full force to ensure the safety of all road users as South Africans travel to various cultural events and gatherings," the statement read.

Motorists are being urged to prioritise safety, whether travelling short or long distances with family and friends. Authorities are reminding the public to obey all traffic laws, drive within speed limits, and refrain from driving under the influence of alcohol or other substances. Ensuring that vehicles are roadworthy before departure is also strongly advised.

Law enforcement also called on communities to respect and preserve heritage sites throughout South Africa. Citizens are encouraged to report any incidents of vandalism or suspicious activity to the nearest police station.

“This Heritage Day, let us honour our diverse cultures and shared history by acting responsibly on the roads and in our communities. Together, we can create a safer and more secure South Africa for everyone,” the statement concluded.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
